title: PTD Today — Media

items.title: Would you go to an in-person dating app event?

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Gen Z is increasingly struggling with loneliness, and dating app Hinge is expanding its One More Hour social impact initiative. Hinge announced a $1 million fund to support social groups in New York, Los Angeles and London to host free or affordable events for young people.
bullets: Gen Z faces rising loneliness according to the description.
Hinge is expanding its One More Hour initiative globally.
A $1 million fund will support social groups to run events.
Target cities are New York, Los Angeles and London.
Events are intended to be free or affordable for young people.
takeaways: No direct technical, grid, or data-center impacts are mentioned in the description.
The initiative is city-focused (NY, LA, London), so any local operational relevance would be limited to those urban markets.
Description frames this as a social program rather than an expansion of Hinge’s digital infrastructure, so immediate network or capacity changes are not indicated.

items.title: Build Knowledge Graph-powered AI Agents For Your Use Case | Databases for AI

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This session demonstrates how to automatically build a knowledge graph from raw documents and connect it to an AI agent so the agent can understand your company's information. It presents an end-to-end workflow that requires no graph expertise and links to a prior episode comparing VectorDB vs GraphDB.
bullets: LMs often lack company-specific knowledge; knowledge graphs are presented as the fix.
Shows automated construction of a knowledge graph from raw documents.
Connects that graph to an AI agent so the agent understands your business data.
Emphasizes an end-to-end build with no need for graph query or graph theory skills.
References a prior episode comparing VectorDB vs GraphDB for Gen AI agents.
takeaways: Enables AI that understands organization-specific information, increasing relevance of outputs for decision-makers.
Automating KG creation reduces reliance on specialized graph-query skills and manual data modeling.
An end-to-end, no-expertise workflow can shorten time-to-value for AI initiatives in your facilities.
The linked episode on VectorDB vs GraphDB may help inform backend choices for integrating knowledge graphs.

items.title: Leveraging tax credits and incentives for sustainability projects

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Randa Barsoum (Tax Partner, PwC US) explains that while energy incentives are often the first thing leaders consider, they are only part of the tax-incentive picture for sustainability. She highlights other opportunities—state and local abatements, location-based incentives, and R&D tax credits tied to innovation in packaging or processes—and urges viewing sustainability investments through a broader tax lens to unlock meaningful tax value.
bullets: Energy incentives are common but not the only tax tools for sustainability.
State and local abatements can support sustainability projects.
Location-based incentives may offer additional tax value for site decisions.
R&D tax credits can apply to innovation efforts like reimagining packaging or processes.
Viewing sustainability investments broadly can reveal meaningful tax benefits.
takeaways: Assess state and local abatements and location-based incentives as part of site selection or expansion planning.
Include tax teams early when evaluating sustainability projects to identify non-energy incentives.
Explore R&D tax credit opportunities for innovation in operational processes that impact infrastructure performance.
Treat sustainability investments as potential drivers of tax value and integrate incentive analysis into project ROI assessments.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description, this video announces the winner of the Summer of RTX r/buildapc Community Build Contest — u/JeffersonPutnam — and showcases their small-form-factor GeForce RTX 5090 system. The description lists the full parts used (Fractal Design Era 2 chassis, GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition, MSI MPG B850I Edge, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, 32GB DDR5-6000, Corsair SF1000, Corsair Nautilus 240 RS) and links to the PCPartPicker parts list and GeForce social channels.
bullets: Winner: u/JeffersonPutnam — SFF GeForce RTX 5090 build from the Summer of RTX contest.
Chassis: Fractal Design Era 2 (small form factor) with MSI MPG B850I Edge motherboard.
CPU/GPU/RAM: Ryzen 7 9800X3D paired with a GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition and 32GB DDR5-6000.
Power and cooling: Corsair SF1000 SFX PSU and Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO were used.
Video host and links provided: GeForce host Dwight (Twitter) and PCPartPicker parts list.
takeaways: A flagship consumer GPU (RTX 5090) can be integrated into a compact SFF chassis — plan for space-constrained layouts when evaluating deployment options.
Power provisioning matters: this build uses a 1000W SFX (Corsair SF1000), highlighting high peak power needs even in small systems.
Thermal management relied on a 240mm AIO (Corsair Nautilus 240 RS), indicating liquid cooling is used to control heat in dense/compact builds.
Motherboard choice (mini-ITX MSI MPG B850I Edge) and component selection reflect trade-offs between performance, size, and integration complexity.

items.title: TTEC accelerates AI deployment and revenue streams with Google Cloud Consulting

ai.summary: Based on the available description, TTEC partnered with Google Cloud Consulting to rapidly prototype and deploy AI solutions, using GenAI and Google Cloud's infrastructure to streamline operations and boost developer productivity. The collaboration helped compress TTEC's digital transformation timeline and unlocked entirely new revenue streams while expanding the company’s ambitions.
bullets: TTEC worked with Google Cloud Consulting to accelerate AI prototyping and deployment.
GenAI and Google Cloud infrastructure were used to streamline operations.
Developer productivity was increased through the partnership and tooling.
The engagement compressed TTEC’s digital transformation timeline.
New revenue streams were unlocked as a result of deployed AI solutions.
takeaways: Partnering with cloud consulting can speed AI deployment and shorten transformation timelines.
Leveraging robust cloud infrastructure supports operational efficiency and higher developer productivity.
GenAI-enabled solutions can create new revenue opportunities for service-oriented businesses.
Cloud consulting engagements can expand organizational ambitions by turning prototypes into production faster.
